fields.title: Classify Ralph state fields for configuration coverage
fields.description: Before implementing `/ralph configure`, define the complete inventory of Ralph persisted-state fields and classify which are user-configurable, system-managed, runtime-only, or derived. This prevents the UI from exposing only tools/agents while missing max iterations, items per iteration, reflection settings, execution profile, sandbox, or future configurable fields.
fields.design: Audit `RalphLoopPersistedState` and the Ralph facade schema. Produce a typed configuration model or helper that enumerates configurable fields. Current configurable set should include: ralph.maxIterations, ralph.itemsPerIteration, ralph.reflectEvery, ralph.reflectInstructions, ralph.pinnedExecutionProfile via explicit recapture/view, ralph.sandboxProfile via explicit recapture/view, capabilityContract.tools user active tool names/snapshot, and capabilityContract.agents enabled agent names/registry fingerprint. Current system/runtime fields should include: taskId, kind, lifecycle, createdAt/updatedAt/startedAt/completedAt/archivedAt, ownership, iteration, lastReflectionAt, pendingDecision, metrics, and system Ralph control tools. Decide whether title/taskFile/task-file content are configurable in this UI or handled by task editing commands, and document that choice in the task.
fields.acceptance_criteria: There is an explicit code-level or documented inventory of every Ralph persisted-state field; each field is classified as configurable, system-managed, runtime-only, derived, or externally edited; `/ralph configure` and the config service tests can assert they cover every configurable field; adding a new Ralph state field requires an intentional classification.
